import io
import string
import list

fn parse_ints(_ s: String) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  let n = string.len(s)
  mut i = 0
  mut cur = 0
  mut sign = 1
  mut has = false
  while i < n
    mut b = 0
    match string.byte_at(s, i)
    when Some(x)
      b = x
    when None
      b = 0
    end
    if b == 32 or b == 9 or b == 10 or b == 13
      if has
        result = list.push(result, cur * sign)
        cur = 0
        sign = 1
        has = false
      end
    else if b == 45
      sign = 0 - 1
    else if b == 43
      sign = 1
    else
      cur = cur * 10 + (b - 48)
      has = true
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  if has
    result = list.push(result, cur * sign)
  end
  result
end

fn get_at(_ xs: List<Int>, _ i: Int) -> Int
  match xs.get(i)
  when Some(x)
    x
  when None
    0
  end
end

fn main() -> Unit
  mut line1 = ""
  mut line2 = ""
  mut have1 = false
  mut have2 = false
  match io.read_line()
  when Some(s)
    line1 = s
    have1 = true
  when None
    have1 = false
  end
  match io.read_line()
  when Some(s)
    line2 = s
    have2 = true
  when None
    have2 = false
  end
  if not have1 or not have2
    print("none")
  else
    let nums = parse_ints(line1)
    match string.parse_int(string.trim(line2))
    when Some(t)
      let n = nums.len()
      mut i = 0
      mut found = false
      mut fi = 0
      mut fj = 0
      while i < n and not found
        mut j = i + 1
        while j < n and not found
          let a = get_at(nums, i)
          let b = get_at(nums, j)
          if a + b == t
            fi = i
            fj = j
            found = true
          end
          j = j + 1
        end
        i = i + 1
      end
      if found
        print("found=#{fi},#{fj}")
      else
        print("none")
      end
    when None
      print("none")
    end
  end
end
